title: iberis bernardiana
intro: Iberis bernardiana, also known as Candytuft, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Asia, characterized by its pink, white, or purple flowers and its ability to thrive in poor soil. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, and is often used to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions. The health benefits of Iberis bernardiana include reducing fever, alleviating coughs and colds, and promoting wound healing. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is sometimes added to salves and creams for topical application.
